[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

revision.text:
[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>

According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|公卿補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>

According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11 deprecated open]]<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河内女王]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

[[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河内女王]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}} [[Category:New]]

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993

In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>

[[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]][[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] <!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター}}, 1993
