[[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[ja:河内女王]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kawachi no Joō]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]]

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|12=draft}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|12=draft}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|12=draft}}, 1993

revision.text:
<!--Kawachi no Joō-->
<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本|12=draft}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子|12=draft}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|12=draft}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|12=draft}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|12=draft}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|12=draft}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|12=draft}}, 1993
<!--Kawachi no Joō-->

At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|12=draft}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|12=draft}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|12=draft}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|12=draft}}, 1993 <!--Kawachi no Joō-->
{{draft categories| [[Category:Kawachi no Joō]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Two pages]] [[Category:Japanese princesses (royalty)]] [[Category:Nara period women]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:8th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:780 deaths]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Kawachi no Joō]] [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]] [[ja:河内女王]] [[en:Kawachi no Joō]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}

<!--Kawachi no Joō-->
<!--Kawachi no Joō--> '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本|12=draft}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子|12=draft}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|12=draft}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|12=draft}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|12=draft}} and was stripped of her rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} (773), her rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|12=draft}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|12=draft}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|12=draft}}, 1993 <!--Kawachi no Joō-->

revision.text:
'''河内女王'''（かわちじょおう/こうちのおおきみ、[[生年不明]] - [[宝亀]]10年[[12月23日 (旧暦)|12月23日]]（[[780年]][[2月5日]]））は、[[奈良時代]]の[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。[[高市皇子]]の皇女<ref name="河内">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref>。[[位階]]は[[正三位]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]11年（[[739年]]）正月、[[粟田女王]]らとともに[[従四位|従四位下]]から[[従四位|従四位上]]に昇叙する<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>。
天平20年（[[748年]]）3月、[[正四位|正四位下]]を授けられる<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref>。同月、[[元正天皇|元正上皇]]の[[難波宮]]行幸に随伴し、[[橘諸兄]]の邸宅で酒宴が催された際に
{{quotation|橘の 下照<small>（したて）</small>る庭に 殿<small>（との）</small>建てて 酒<small>（さか）</small>みづきいます 我<small>（わ）</small>が大君<small>（おほきみ）</small>かも
（橘の 木陰も輝く庭に 御殿を建てて 酒盛りをなさる わが大君よ<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref>） }} という歌を詠んでいる。なおこの歌は、季節からして、天平16年夏に、聖武天皇らが難波宮に行幸した際の歌だという説もある<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>。
[[淳仁天皇|淳仁朝]]の[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）8月、[[従三位]]<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref>、同4年（[[760年]]）5月、[[正三位]]にすすむ<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>。
のち[[神護景雲]]3年（[[769年]]）の[[不破内親王]]の事件に連坐したものか、無位とされており、[[光仁天皇|光仁朝]]の[[宝亀]]4年（[[773年]]）正月、不破内親王が本位四品に復したのと同時に、正三位に復した<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref>。宝亀10年12月23日薨去<ref name="河内"/>。高市皇子は[[持統天皇]]10年（[[696年]]）に没しているので、河内女王の年齢は少なく見積もってもこの時点で83歳以上の高齢である。なお、『[[公卿補任]]』宝字四年条によると、「河内王」という名のほぼ同年齢の皇族がいて、[[天武天皇]]8年生まれとなっているようで、そこからすると、河内女王は元正天皇と同年齢の従姉妹となり、上記の歌の頃は65歳だった、ということになる<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]] *[[天平]]11年（[[739年]]）正月13日：[[従四位|従四位上]] *天平20年（[[748年]]）3月22日：[[正四位|正四位下]] *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）8月1日、[[従三位]] *時期不詳：[[正三位]] *[[神護景雲]]3年（[[769年]]）5月29日以降?：[[無位]] *[[宝亀]]4年（[[773年]]）正月1日：正三位（復位） *宝亀10年（[[779年]]）12月23日：薨去
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 [[岩波書店]]、1990年 * 『続日本紀』3 新日本古典文学大系14 岩波書店、1992年 * 『続日本紀』4 新日本古典文学大系15 岩波書店、1995年 * 『続日本紀』5 新日本古典文学大系16 岩波書店、1998年 *[[宇治谷孟]]訳『続日本紀 （上）・（中）・（下）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年、1995年 * 『萬葉集』（二）完訳日本の古典3、[[小学館]]、1984年 * 『萬葉集』（六）完訳日本の古典7、小学館、1987年 *『日本古代人名辞典』2 - p577、[[竹内理三]]・[[山田英雄]]・[[平野邦雄]]編、[[吉川弘文館]]、1959年 *『日本女性人名辞典』p338、[[芳賀登]]・[[一番ヶ瀬康子]]・[[中嶌邦]]・[[祖田浩一]]編、[[日本図書センター]]、1993年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かわちしよおう}} [[Category:日本の女王 (皇族)]] [[Category:奈良時代の女性皇族]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:正三位受位者]] [[Category:8世紀日本の女性皇族]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:780年没]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[河内女王]] to [[Kawachi no Joō]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
'''河内女王'''（かわちじょおう/こうちのおおきみ、[[生年不明]] - [[宝亀]]10年[[12月23日 (旧暦)|12月23日]]（[[780年]][[2月5日]]））は、[[奈良時代]]の[[日本]]の[[皇族]]。[[高市皇子]]の皇女<ref name="河内">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10年12月23日条</ref>。[[位階]]は[[正三位]]。
== 経歴 == [[聖武天皇|聖武朝]]の[[天平]]11年（[[739年]]）正月、[[粟田女王]]らとともに[[従四位|従四位下]]から[[従四位|従四位上]]に昇叙する<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11年正月13日条</ref>。
天平20年（[[748年]]）3月、[[正四位|正四位下]]を授けられる<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20年3月22日条</ref>。同月、[[元正天皇|元正上皇]]の[[難波宮]]行幸に随伴し、[[橘諸兄]]の邸宅で酒宴が催された際に
{{quotation|橘の 下照<small>（したて）</small>る庭に 殿<small>（との）</small>建てて 酒<small>（さか）</small>みづきいます 我<small>（わ）</small>が大君<small>（おほきみ）</small>かも
（橘の 木陰も輝く庭に 御殿を建てて 酒盛りをなさる わが大君よ<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref>） }} という歌を詠んでいる。なおこの歌は、季節からして、天平16年夏に、聖武天皇らが難波宮に行幸した際の歌だという説もある<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>。
[[淳仁天皇|淳仁朝]]の[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）8月、[[従三位]]<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2年8月1日条</ref>、同4年（[[760年]]）5月、[[正三位]]にすすむ<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4年5月3日条</ref>。
のち[[神護景雲]]3年（[[769年]]）の[[不破内親王]]の事件に連坐したものか、無位とされており、[[光仁天皇|光仁朝]]の[[宝亀]]4年（[[773年]]）正月、不破内親王が本位四品に復したのと同時に、正三位に復した<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4年正月1日条</ref>。宝亀10年12月23日薨去<ref name="河内"/>。高市皇子は[[持統天皇]]10年（[[696年]]）に没しているので、河内女王の年齢は少なく見積もってもこの時点で83歳以上の高齢である。なお、『[[公卿補任]]』宝字四年条によると、「河内王」という名のほぼ同年齢の皇族がいて、[[天武天皇]]8年生まれとなっているようで、そこからすると、河内女王は元正天皇と同年齢の従姉妹となり、上記の歌の頃は65歳だった、ということになる<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>。
== 官歴 == 『[[続日本紀]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]] *[[天平]]11年（[[739年]]）正月13日：[[従四位|従四位上]] *天平20年（[[748年]]）3月22日：[[正四位|正四位下]] *[[天平宝字]]2年（[[758年]]）8月1日、[[従三位]] *時期不詳：[[正三位]] *[[神護景雲]]3年（[[769年]]）5月29日以降?：[[無位]] *[[宝亀]]4年（[[773年]]）正月1日：正三位（復位） *宝亀10年（[[779年]]）12月23日：薨去
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『続日本紀』2 新日本古典文学大系13 [[岩波書店]]、1990年 * 『続日本紀』3 新日本古典文学大系14 岩波書店、1992年 * 『続日本紀』4 新日本古典文学大系15 岩波書店、1995年 * 『続日本紀』5 新日本古典文学大系16 岩波書店、1998年 *[[宇治谷孟]]訳『続日本紀 （上）・（中）・（下）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1992年、1995年 * 『萬葉集』（二）完訳日本の古典3、[[小学館]]、1984年 * 『萬葉集』（六）完訳日本の古典7、小学館、1987年 *『日本古代人名辞典』2 - p577、[[竹内理三]]・[[山田英雄]]・[[平野邦雄]]編、[[吉川弘文館]]、1959年 *『日本女性人名辞典』p338、[[芳賀登]]・[[一番ヶ瀬康子]]・[[中嶌邦]]・[[祖田浩一]]編、[[日本図書センター]]、1993年
{{DEFAULTSORT:かわちしよおう}} [[Category:日本の女王 (皇族)]] [[Category:奈良時代の女性皇族]] [[Category:万葉歌人]] [[Category:正三位受位者]] [[Category:8世紀日本の女性皇族]] [[Category:7世紀生]] [[Category:780年没]]

revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:Kawachi no Joō}}

'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}. == References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Shoten|qq=}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogakukan|qq=}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

[[sn:Kawachi no Joō]] [[ja:%E6%B2%B3%E5%86%85%E5%A5%B3%E7%8E%8B]] [[ja:河内女王]] [[en:Kawachi no Joō]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Fuha Incident]] → [[Princess Fuwa]]
revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|qq=}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese|qq=}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takano|qq=}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō|qq=}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmu|qq=}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junnin|qq=}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō Hōji|qq=}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingō Keiun|qq=}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|qq=}} (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Shoten|qq=}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogakukan|qq=}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|qq=}} 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a {{ill|Japanese|ja|日本|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese|qq=}} princess during the {{ill|Nara period|ja|奈良時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nara period|qq=}}. She was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Takano|ja|高市皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takano|qq=}}.<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō|qq=}} era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied {{ill|Empress Genmei|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genmei|qq=}} on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of {{ill|Tachibana no Moroe|ja|橘諸兄|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tachibana no Moroe|qq=}}. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor {{ill|Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shōmu|qq=}} to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor {{ill|Junnin|ja|淳仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junnin|qq=}}, Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the {{ill|Tenpyō Hōji|ja|天平宝字|1=Tenpyō-hōji|2=ja|3=天平宝字|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenpyō Hōji|qq=}} era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the {{ill|Jingō Keiun|ja|神護景雲|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingō Keiun|qq=}} era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|qq=}} (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of {{ill|Jitō|ja|持統天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jitō|qq=}} (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shoku Nihongi|qq=}}: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanami Shoten|ja|岩波書店|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanami Shoten|qq=}}, 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=}} 3, {{ill|Shogakukan|ja|小学館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shogakukan|qq=}}, 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. (The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}

During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>

She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.

In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Nara period women]] [[Category:Man'yō poets]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] [[Category:8th-century Japanese women]] [[Category:7th-century births]] [[Category:780 deaths]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Nara period women
revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
