revision.comment: Merge [[Category:正三位受位者]] into [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:奈良時代の女性皇族]] into [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members of the Nara period]] via bot
revision.text:
'''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q96714020]] Wikidata name: Kawachi-joō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=}}, 1993

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0780|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|father={{ill|Prince Takechi|qid=Q837590}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q96714020]] Wikidata name: Kawachi-joō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=|1=Naniwa Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3608523}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系|1=Nihon koten bungaku taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507508}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集|1=Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507506}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507561}}, 1993

== Notes ==

== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系|1=Nihon koten bungaku taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507508}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集|1=Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507506}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507561}}, 1993

<!--Kawachi no Joō--><!--Kawachi no Joō--><!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[Category:Japanese Female Royalty in the 8th Century]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members of the Nara period]] [[Category:Princesses of Imperial Japan]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

revision.comment: Bot: append |1= |comment= |WD= in {{ill}}
revision.text:
{{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0780|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|father={{ill|Prince Takechi|qid=Q837590}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q96714020]] Wikidata name: Kawachi-joō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=|1=Naniwa Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3608523}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系|1=Nihon koten bungaku taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507508}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集|1=Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507506}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507561}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0780|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072}}|father={{ill|Prince Takechi|qid=Q837590}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q96714020]] Wikidata name: Kawachi-joō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=|1=Naniwa Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3608523}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系|1=Nihon koten bungaku taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507508}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集|1=Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507506}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|qq=|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|qq=|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507561}}, 1993
{{translated page|ja|河内女王|version=98193576|comment=Imported full ja history}}

During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>

However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:河内女王]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0780|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|father={{ill|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q96714020]] Wikidata name: Kawachi-joō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|qq=|1=Naniwa Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3608523}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系|1=Nihon koten bungaku taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507508}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集|1=Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507506}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507561}}, 1993

<!--Kawachi no Joō--><!--Kawachi no Joō--><!--Kawachi no Joō--> [[Category:Japanese Female Royalty in the 8th Century]] [[Category:Female Imperial Family Members of the Nara period]] [[Category:Princesses of Imperial Japan]] [[Category:Recipients of Senior Third Rank]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q96714020}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:河内女王]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0650|death_date={{death date|0780|2|5}}|gender={{ill|female|WD=Q6581072}}|father={{ill|Prince Takechi|WD=Q837590}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q96714020]] Wikidata name: Kawachi-joō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:17, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:06, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kawachi Joō''' (河内女王, dates of birth unknown - [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] 10, 12th month, 23rd day [February 5, 780]) was a [[:en:Japan|Japanese]] princess during the [[:en:Nara period|Nara period]]. She was the daughter of [[:en:Prince Takechi|Prince Takano]].<ref name="Kawachi">『続日本紀』巻第三十五、光仁天皇 宝亀10th year 12月23日条</ref> She held the court Rank of {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}}.
== Career == In the eleventh year of the [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] era (739), Kawachi Joō was promoted from {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|従四位下|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|1=Junior Fourth Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} to {{ill|Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|ja|従四位上|1=Junior Fourth Rank|lt=Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 従四位}} alongside Princess Awata.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十三、聖武天皇 天平11th year 正月13日条</ref>
In the 20th year of Tenpyō (748), she was elevated to {{ill|Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|ja|正四位下|1=Senior Fourth Rank|lt=Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Fourth Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 正四位}}.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第十七、聖武天皇 天平20th year 3月22日条</ref> During the same month, she accompanied [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genmei]] on her visit to {{ill|Naniwa Palace|ja|難波宮|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naniwa Palace|1=Naniwa Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3608523}}, where a feast was held at the residence of [[:en:Tachibana no Moroe|Tachibana no Moroe]]. At this event, she composed the following poem:
{{quotation| 橘の 下照（したて）る庭に 殿（との）建てて 酒（さか）みづきいます 我（わ）が大君（おほきみ）かも
(The garden, where the mandarin orange trees glow, hosts a splendid palace where our great lord holds a feast.)<ref>『万葉集』巻第十八、4059番</ref> }}
There is another theory suggesting that this poem was written during a summer visit of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Shōmu]] to Naniwa Palace in the 16th year of Tenpyō.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（二）393頁注1</ref><ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）25頁注2</ref>
During the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Junnin|Junnin]], Kawachi Joō was promoted to {{ill|Junior Third Rank|ja|従三位|1=Junior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Junior Third Rank|qq=}} in the second year of the [[:en:Tenpyō-hōji|Tenpyō-hōji]] era (758)<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字2nd year 8月1日条</ref> and advanced to {{ill|Senior Third Rank|ja|正三位|1=Senior Third Rank|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/Senior Third Rank|qq=}} in the fourth year (760).<ref>『続日本紀』巻第二十二、廃帝 淳仁天皇 天平宝字4th year 5月3日条</ref>
Later, in the third year of the [[:en:Jingo-keiun|Jingō Keiun]] era (769), Kawachi Joō appears to have been implicated in the {{ill|Princess Fuwa|lt=Princess Fuwa|lt=Fuha Incident|ja|不破内親王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuha Incident|qq=}} and was stripped of her Rank. However, in the fourth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] (773), her Rank was reinstated to Senior Third Rank, coinciding with the restoration of Princess Fuha's Rank.<ref>『続日本紀』巻第三十二、光仁天皇 宝亀4th year 正月1日条</ref> She passed away on the 23rd day of the 12th month of Hōki 10 (779).<ref name="Kawachi"/> Given that Prince Takano died in the 10th year of [[:en:Empress Jitō|Jitō]] (696), Kawachi Joō must have been at least 83 years old at the time of her death. According to the {{ill|Kugyō Bunin|ja|Kugyō補任|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kugyō Bunin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a contemporary royal family member named "Kawachi Ō" (河内王) was of nearly the same age, possibly born in the eighth year of Emperor {{ill|Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}}, making Kawachi Joō a cousin of Empress Genmei and about 65 years old at the time of the aforementioned poem.<ref>小学館『萬葉集』（六）27頁注2</ref>
== Court Ranks == As recorded in the [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]]: * Unknown date: Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō 11 (739), 1st month, 13th day: Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade * Tenpyō 20 (748), 3rd month, 22nd day: Senior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade * Tenpyō Hōji 2 (758), 8th month, 1st day: Junior Third Rank * Unknown date: Senior Third Rank * Jingō Keiun 3 (769), after 5th month, 29th day: Stripped of Rank * Hōki 4 (773), 1st month, 1st day: Senior Third Rank (restored) * Hōki 10 (779), 12th month, 23rd day: Passed away
== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== References == * 『続日本紀』2, {{ill|Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja|新日本古典文学大系|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学大系|1=Nihon koten bungaku taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507508}}, [[:en:Iwanami Shoten|Iwanami Shoten]], 1990 * 『続日本紀』3, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1992 * 『続日本紀』4, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1995 * 『続日本紀』5, Shin Nihon Koten Bungaku Taikei, Iwanami Shoten, 1998 * {{ill|Ujitani Mitsuru|ja|宇治谷孟|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujitani Mitsuru|qq=}}, *Shoku Nihongi (Volumes 1–3)*, Kodansha Academic Library, 1992, 1995 * 『萬葉集』（二）, {{ill|Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja|完訳日本の古典|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本古典文学全集|1=Nihon Koten Bungaku Zenshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507506}} 3, [[:en:Shogakukan|Shogakukan]], 1984 * 『萬葉集』（六）, Perfect Translations of Japanese Classics 7, Shogakukan, 1987 * 『日本古代人名辞典』2, {{ill|Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|ja|吉川弘文館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|1=Yoshikawa Kōbunkan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q4346755}}, 1959 * 『日本女性人名辞典』, {{ill|Nihon Tosho Center|ja|日本図書センター|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Tosho Center|1=Nihon Tosho Sentā|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11507561}}, 1993
